title: Go HTTP Upload and Download Gateways · Runtime Contract: Maxbytes Multipart Reader
summary: A Go handler buffers multipart forms and S3 objects in memory, trusts proxy headers, writes status too early, and continues transferring after the client leaves. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the maxbytes multipart reader increment in streaming Go file-gateway handler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use MaxBytesReader or bounded readers and streaming multipart iteration without ParseMultipartForm disk or memory surprises.
Apply trusted authority, authentication, authorization, method, route, headers, body, multipart, rate, and concurrency admission before storage work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Maxbytes Multipart Reader · Theory Maxbytes Multipart Reader
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 1719 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a streaming Go file handler on request admission and goroutine cleanup; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use MaxBytesReader or bounded readers and streaming multipart iteration without ParseMultipartForm disk or memory surprises.
